join_order.notes: Load sub/fy.bin as flat int16_t[86135] (172KB, L1-resident). Build DenseBitmap(86135) marking adsh_codes where fy==2023. 26612 qualifying adsh_codes (~31%).

join_order.build: pre(filtered: stmt='IS' AND adsh in fy2023_bitmap), partitioned into N_PARTS=64 sub-maps
join_order.notes: Three-phase build eliminates serial merge bottleneck: Phase A (parallel): 64 threads scan pre disjoint ranges, emit (key,pre_row) into thread-local per-partition buffers Buffers[tid][part] — zero contention. Phase B (parallel): thread p merges Buffers[0..63][p] into partition_map[p] in thread-ascending order (preserving min pre_row per key). Result: 64 CompactHashMaps, each ~8594 expected entries, 262KB each, 16.8MB total — fits in L3. Probe in main_scan: compute part=(fibonacci_hash(key) >> 58) & 63, find in partition_map[part]. Each partition stays L3-resident during probe phase.

data_structures.sub_fy_array: int16_t[86135] direct array (172KB, L1-resident) — indexed by adsh_code
data_structures.phase_a_buffers: std::vector<KeyRow>[n_threads][N_PARTS] where KeyRow={uint64_t key, uint32_t pre_row} — 2D buffer, thread t writes only to Buffers[t][*], zero contention. N_PARTS=64. Each Buffers[t][p] reserved for ~200 entries = 1.6KB. Total ~4.4MB.
data_structures.partition_maps: CompactHashMap<uint64_t,uint32_t>[N_PARTS] — N_PARTS=64 sub-maps. Each sub-map: expected=10000, capacity=16384, size=16384×16B=262KB. Total=64×262KB=16.8MB fits in L3 (44MB). Partition function: part = (key * 0x9E3779B97F4A7C15ULL) >> 58 (top 6 bits = 64 partitions). Each partition holds first pre_row per (adsh_code,tagver_code) key for IS+fy2023 rows.
data_structures.thread_local_agg: CompactHashMap<uint64_t,AggEntry>(expected=8192) per thread — capacity=16384×28B=458KB/thread, 64×458KB=29MB total (L3-resident). Key=(adsh_code<<32|pre_row), value={double sum_value, int64_t cnt}.
data_structures.final_reagg: CompactHashMap<uint64_t,ReAggEntry>(expected=250000) — keyed on FNV-64 of composite string (name||0x01||tag||0x01||plabel). No heap string allocs. ReAggEntry={double sum_value, int64_t cnt, uint32_t sample_adsh_code, uint32_t sample_pre_row}. Capacity=524288×36B=18.9MB, L3-resident.

parallelism.pre_scan_phase_a: PARALLEL OMP: 64 threads, schedule(static), each scans disjoint pre row range. Output: per-thread per-partition buffers Buffers[tid][part]. Zero cross-thread writes.
parallelism.pre_merge_phase_b: PARALLEL OMP: 64 threads, thread p merges columns Buffers[0..63][p] into partition_map[p]. Process threads in ascending order (t=0..63) to preserve min pre_row per key. Each thread merges ~8594 entries into its own 262KB sub-map — no synchronization needed.
parallelism.num_scan_phase: PARALLEL OMP: morsel-driven over 395 num blocks (100K rows each), atomic next_block counter. Each thread probes partitioned maps (partition_maps[part].find(key)).
shared_read_only: pre columns (for fy2023_bitmap build and duplicate forward scan)

build_phase_detail.step1_setup: Load sub/fy.bin (172KB). Build int16_t sub_fy[86135]. Build DenseBitmap fy2023_bits(86135). Load usd_code and is_code from dict files.
build_phase_detail.step2_phase_a_parallel_collect: OMP parallel, schedule(static): thread t scans pre rows [t*chunk...(t+1)*chunk). For row i where pre_stmt[i]==is_code AND fy2023_bits.test(pre_adsh[i]): compute key=(adsh_code<<32|tagver_code), part=(key*0x9E3779B97F4A7C15ULL)>>58 (top 6 bits, giving 0..63). Push {key,(uint32_t)i} into Buffers[t][part]. Each thread only writes to its own row — no mutex, no atomic.
build_phase_detail.step3_phase_b_parallel_merge: Allocate partition_maps[64] each CompactHashMap(10000). OMP parallel: thread p iterates t=0..63 in order; for each {key,pre_row} in Buffers[t][p]: if partition_maps[p].find(key)==nullptr then partition_maps[p].insert(key,pre_row). Processing t in ascending order guarantees first (smallest) pre_row is kept per key. Thread p owns partition_maps[p] exclusively — no contention.
build_phase_detail.duplicate_handling: For the ~0.7% of keys with multiple IS+fy2023 pre rows: during main_scan, after finding first_pre_row via partition_maps, linearly scan forward in pre (pre_adsh[r]==adsh AND pre_tagver[r]==tagver AND pre_stmt[r]==is_code) to accumulate additional pre rows. Pre is sorted by (adsh_code,tagver_code), so duplicates are contiguous.
build_phase_detail.partition_map_sizes: N_PARTS=64, total expected=550374, per partition expected=~8600, capacity per partition=16384 entries × 16B = 262KB. Total memory = 64×262KB = 16.8MB.

indexes.layout: uint32_t n_blocks; then n_blocks x ZoneMap{int8_t min_uom, int8_t max_uom, int16_t _pad, int32_t min_ddate, int32_t max_ddate} = 12 bytes each

aggregation_detail.phase2_key: FNV-64 hash of composite string: name_sv || 0x01 || tag_string || 0x01 || plabel_sv. CORRECTNESS: tag_string is decoded via tag_data[tag_offsets[tagver_code]..tag_offsets[tagver_code+1]] where tagver_code=pre_tagver[pre_row]. Different tagver_codes with same tag string (same tag, different version) must merge into same group. ReAggEntry stores sample_adsh_code and sample_pre_row for final string decode of top-200 rows only.
aggregation_detail.phase2_reagg_map: CompactHashMap<uint64_t,ReAggEntry>(expected=250000) — FNV-64 keyed, no string heap allocs during reagg loop. Zero heap allocation during aggregation.
aggregation_detail.phase2_topk: partial_sort top-200 by sum_value DESC on flat vector of all ReAggEntry items. Decode strings (name, tag, plabel) only for the final ≤200 output rows using stored sample_adsh_code and sample_pre_row.

output.topk_strategy: partial_sort on all reagg entries then resize to 200

optimization_notes: Root cause is Category B (algorithm change): the serial_merge_build_is_map phase (3654ms, 70% of total) uses a single thread to merge 550K entries from 64 per-thread vectors into a global CompactHashMap. After the 86MB parallel OMP pre scan evicts the map from L3, and with 3/4 of thread-local vector data on remote NUMA nodes, each serial merge step pays 150-300ns in NUMA-remote and DRAM-cold latency — making the overall merge dominated by latency rather than throughput. Iter_4 confirmed that even a single-threaded sequential pre scan takes a similar 3858ms, ruling out the collect phase as the fix. The solution is to eliminate the serial merge entirely via a three-phase partitioned build: (A) parallel collect into per-thread per-partition buffers Buffers[tid][part] — zero cross-thread writes; (B) parallel merge where thread p owns and builds partition_map[p] from Buffers[*][p] in thread-ascending order (guaranteeing minimum pre_row per key) — 64 fully independent merges; (C) probe using partition_maps[part] during main_scan. Each of 64 sub-maps is ~262KB (near L2 size), and all 64 together total 16.8MB which stays in the 44MB L3 throughout main_scan — cutting probe latency from ~100ns (DRAM) to ~10ns (L3). Additionally incorporates iter_4's proven improvements: thread_local_agg sized to CompactHashMap(8192) for 458KB/thread (L2-resident, 29MB total in L3) and FNV-64 keyed final reagg (no string heap allocations, decode strings only for top-200 rows) which together reduce the reagg+decode phase from 457ms to ~268ms.
